position_summary: Claude’s final position is mostly verified: the §4 Control-2 prose now reports the lower and upper tails correctly, avoids the old 'rarer-than-chance' overclaim, and preserves the substrate-inherited stringency reading. The only remaining issue I see is not in the §4 prose but in the revised analysis code comments, which still describe the per-gene cardinality value as a decomposition/mechanism more strongly than the computation supports.

findings.title: Control-2 prose is fixed, but analysis-code comment still overstates the cardinality metric as a decomposition
findings.evidence: MISMATCH in code comment / HOLDS in manuscript prose: §4 now says the measured per-gene concentration effect is small and that the mechanism of the lower-tail direction is not interpreted further (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:95-100). The artifact fields HOLDS: signed_z -5.645, empirical_p_lower 0.0005, empirical_p_upper 1.0, empirical_p_two_sided 0.001, and mean_distinct_C_diseases_per_gene_observed/null 2.388/2.442 exist in docs/manuscript/analysis/sensitivity_results.json:21-28. But the revised code comment still calls the cardinality metric 'the decomposition F-001 asks for' and says true-label rows 'tend to share ONE disease' while permutation 'disperses them and raises it' (docs/manuscript/analysis/sensitivity_panel.py:95-113). That comment overstates a single per-passing-gene summary as a decomposition and is stronger than the manuscript’s now-calibrated interpretation.
findings.concern_path: A hostile reviewer who opens the analysis script can argue that the primary analysis artifact still embeds the causal story that §4 now carefully avoids. The numerical result remains traceable and the manuscript reading is defensible, but the code provenance is not as clean as the prose.
findings.suggested_action: Revise the comment to match the manuscript: return the distinct passing-pair count plus one descriptive per-passing-gene cardinality summary; do not call it a decomposition or imply it explains the lower-tail direction.

escalated_findings.rationale: Partially addressed in §4 but replaced by F-012 at lower severity: the manuscript no longer interprets the mechanism beyond the measured small cardinality difference (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:95-100 HOLDS), but the analysis script comment still overstates the metric as a decomposition/explanation (docs/manuscript/analysis/sensitivity_panel.py:95-113 MISMATCH).

dropped_findings.rationale: HOLDS fixed in §4 and artifact: Control-2 prose no longer claims real disease associations explain observed < null via fewer diseases per gene; it reports 2.39 vs 2.44 and declines to interpret mechanism (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:95-100). New artifact fields include the cardinality means (docs/manuscript/analysis/sensitivity_results.json:27-28).

HOLDS fixed: sensitivity_results.json now stores signed_z -5.645, empirical_p_lower 0.0005, empirical_p_upper 1.0, and empirical_p_two_sided 0.001 (docs/manuscript/analysis/sensitivity_results.json:21-25), and §4 reports lower-tail p≈5e-4 with upper-tail p=1.0 (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:92-95).

HOLDS fixed: Control-2 heading is now 'stringency is substrate-inherited, and its pass count is label-dependent' (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:83), avoiding the prior 'rubber stamp' framing.

HOLDS fixed: the problematic §4.3 phrase is now 'polarity marker-checked' (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:146-148). Remaining 'validated' instances are in the calibration warning/self-audit discussion, not claim text (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:4-5,207-212).

HOLDS fixed: §4.6 scopes the corrected locus-artifact result to the cluster-membership artifact and explicitly leaves the GWAS disease-label LD question open (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:231-234). §4.4 likewise says the cis check strengthens the perturbation-specific case and does not prove the disease link (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:182-185).
HOLDS fixed: §4.6 now says not every member checked every number, but each headline number was re-derived by at least one clean-room member (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:220-225).
HOLDS fixed: §4 now opens with 'We report six results' (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:19).

HOLDS fixed: §1 no longer says the Control-2 result 'proves' inherited stringency; it says the disease-hop stringency is largely inherited from the enrichment data (docs/manuscript/sections/01_introduction.md:69-72).

HOLDS fixed: §4.1 now specifies that §4.1b supplies a disease-hop support-rate control over the full A×C space, not a ledger accuracy rate or precision/recall claim (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:68-70).

HOLDS fixed: §4.4 separates the main genome-wide DE row from the three corroborating checks, saying those checks are drawn from study QC fields and finding analysis rather than the same DE row (docs/manuscript/sections/04_results.md:175-182).

recommended_next_question: Fix the stale Control-2 code comment so the analysis artifact and §4 prose carry the same scoped interpretation; after that, debate can converge.
